context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the default list sorting behavior
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The indices list must be sorted in strictly ascending numerical order without duplicates.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_conditional_projection.json.description: Project a record batch through a compiled conditional expression. The schema has two equally-typed signed-integer columns. The expression compares the two columns element-wise and, for each row, selects the value from the first column when it is strictly greater than the second, otherwise the value from the second column (an if/then/else over a greater-than comparison). The adapter reports the operation, the declared result type, the number of output rows, and the resulting projected column as an ordered value list.

feature2_arithmetic_projection.json.description: Project a record batch through a compiled arithmetic expression. The schema has two double-precision floating-point columns. The expression adds the two columns element-wise to produce a new double-precision column. The adapter reports the operation, the declared result type, the number of output rows, and the resulting projected column as an ordered value list.

feature3_comparison_filter.json.description: Filter a record batch with a compiled boolean condition that compares a column against a constant. The schema has one double-precision column. The condition keeps rows whose value is strictly less than a fixed numeric literal. Filtering yields a selection vector of zero-based row positions (an unsigned 32-bit index list) identifying the surviving rows in ascending order. The adapter reports the operation, the selection index element type, the number of surviving rows, and the selected positions as an ordered index list.

feature4_membership_filter.json.description: Filter a record batch with a compiled membership condition that keeps rows whose value belongs to a fixed set of candidate constants (an IN-set test). The single column may be textual or signed integer; the candidate set is supplied as part of the expression and is typed to match the column. Filtering yields a selection vector of zero-based positions (an unsigned 32-bit index list) of the matching rows in ascending order. The adapter reports the operation, the selection index element type, the number of matching rows, and the matching positions as an ordered index list.

feature5_boolean_composition_filter.json.description: Filter a record batch with a compiled boolean condition built from several elementary comparisons combined with boolean conjunction and disjunction. The schema has two double-precision columns. The condition keeps a row when EITHER (the first column is below a constant AND the first column exceeds the second column) OR (the second column is below another constant). Filtering yields a selection vector of zero-based positions (an unsigned 32-bit index list) of surviving rows in ascending order. The adapter reports the operation, the selection index element type, the number of surviving rows, and the positions as an ordered index list.

feature6_pattern_match_projection.json.description: Project a record batch through a compiled SQL-style pattern-matching expression. The schema has one textual column. The expression applies a LIKE match against a pattern literal, where the percent sign is a wildcard matching any (possibly empty) run of characters, producing a boolean column that is true exactly for the rows whose text matches the pattern. The adapter reports the operation, the declared result type, the number of output rows, and the resulting boolean column as an ordered value list of lowercase truth values.

cases.expected_output:
op=project
result_type=bool
length=4
values=[false, true, true, true]

feature7_filter_then_project.json.description: Run a two-stage filter-then-project pipeline over a single record batch. The schema has three signed-integer columns. A filter condition first selects the rows where the first column exceeds the second column, producing a selection vector. A separate projection expression is then evaluated only over those selected rows: for each surviving row it selects the second column when that column is below the third column, otherwise the third column (an if/then/else over a less-than comparison). The third column may contain a missing value, which propagates through the comparison and projection as a missing output. The adapter reports the operation, the declared result type, the number of projected rows, and the projected values as an ordered list with missing entries rendered as a null marker.

feature8_typed_literals.json.description: Construct constant (literal) nodes of every supported scalar type and validate type/value compatibility. Each literal is described by a target type and a value; the target type may be given either as a structured type object or by its canonical type name (both forms must be accepted). A literal whose value is compatible with the declared type is accepted; a literal whose value does not match the declared type, or whose declared type is missing, is rejected. The adapter reports, for each requested literal in order, the type label, which type-spelling form was used, and whether construction succeeded or failed, with failures carrying a normalized error category.

cases.expected_output:
op=literal_check
type=int32 form=datatype status=ok
type=float64 form=datatype status=ok
type=string form=datatype status=ok
type=int64 form=name status=ok
type=int64 form=datatype status=error error=type_mismatch
type=none form=datatype status=error error=type_mismatch

feature9_null_node_rejection.json.description: Reject expression-tree constructions in which a required child node is missing (supplied as a null). Whenever a node position that demands a sub-expression is given a null, the construction is refused rather than producing a malformed tree. This is checked across every position that takes a node: a field reference, the branches of an if/then/else, the operands of a boolean conjunction or disjunction, the subject of a membership test, an operand of a comparison function, the root expression of a projection, and the condition of a filter. The adapter reports a single normalized error category for each such construction.

_constraints.input_condition: Every test case output evaluation
_constraints.output_rule: All lines in the expected output must end with a newline character.

_constraints.input_condition: Input columns contain null values during projection
_constraints.output_rule: The projected value list must render null entries as the literal string 'null' without quotes, lowercase.

_constraints.input_condition: Input columns contain null values during filtering
_constraints.output_rule: This represents a behavioral constraint for null propagation in the selection logic (implied by Feature 7 description), though specific index formatting is not noted in provided test cases.

_constraints.input_condition: Input uses 'form' field with value 'name' or 'datatype'
_constraints.output_rule: The adapter reports the exact spelling used in the input for the 'form' field.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'literal_check'
_constraints.output_rule: The status must be 'ok' for compatible literals and 'error' for incompatible ones (e.g., string value for int64 type, or null type).

_constraints.input_condition: Literal check fails due to type mismatch or null type
_constraints.output_rule: The error category for failed checks must be the literal string 'type_mismatch'.

_constraints.input_condition: Expression or condition is explicitly set to null (missing required child node)
_constraints.output_rule: The operation must report a single line with error code 'null_node' and no other text.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'literal_check' with multiple literals
_constraints.output_rule: The output must list exactly one result object (type, form, status, optional error) per input literal in the order they were provided.

_constraints.input_condition: Input literal has type set to null, regardless of value
_constraints.output_rule: The reported type in the output line must be the literal string 'none'.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'literal_check' and status is 'error'
_constraints.output_rule: The output line must include the 'error' field with the category.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'literal_check' and status is 'ok'
_constraints.output_rule: The output line must not include an 'error' field.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'project' and result_type is 'bool'
_constraints.output_rule: The 'values' array must contain the strings 'true' or 'false' in lowercase, not JSON booleans.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'project' with function 'like' on string column
_constraints.output_rule: The pattern '%...' (wildcard) must match any run of characters, including empty runs (start/end of string).

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'filter'
_constraints.output_rule: The 'indices' list must be in strictly ascending order (zero-based positions).

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'filter' and condition involves a comparison with a null
_constraints.output_rule: Null values in the column must not satisfy the condition (e.g., null <= 5 should be false, resulting in null rows being excluded from the index list).

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'filter_project' and input column is null
_constraints.output_rule: The final projected value must be 'null' (as per Feature 7 description).

_constraints.input_condition: Any expression node requires a child but receives null
_constraints.output_rule: Rejection applies to: field references, if/then/else branches, boolean and/or operands, IN-set subject, comparison function operands, projection root, filter condition.

_constraints.input_condition: Input literal has 'type': null
_constraints.output_rule: Status must be 'error' with 'error': 'type_mismatch'.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'literal_check'
_constraints.output_rule: The 'form' field in output must exactly match the input 'form' field value.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'literal_check'
_constraints.output_rule: The 'type' field in output must exactly match the input 'type' field value (or 'none' if null input).

_constraints.input_condition: Literal value is compatible with declared type
_constraints.output_rule: Status must be 'ok'.

_constraints.input_condition: Literal value is incompatible with declared type (e.g., string 'hello' for int64)
_constraints.output_rule: Status must be 'error' with 'error': 'type_mismatch'.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'project' or 'filter' with float64 columns
_constraints.output_rule: Arithmetic and comparison operations must follow IEEE 754 double precision rules; exact equality checks on floats are generally false unless bits are identical.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'project' or 'filter' with int32 columns
_constraints.output_rule: Strict greater-than/less-than comparisons must use signed 32-bit integer semantics (wrapping behavior on wrap-around not explicitly handled in tests, but implied by type).

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'filter' with 'in' node and string column
_constraints.output_rule: Membership test ('in' node) must use exact string equality for all candidates in the set.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'project' with 'if' node
_constraints.output_rule: For each row, evaluate the condition; if true, take 'then' value; if false, take 'else' value. Nulls in arguments propagate through selection.

_constraints.input_condition: Input literal has 'type' as a type name string (e.g., 'int64') instead of object
_constraints.output_rule: Status must be 'ok' if the name maps to a valid type.

_constraints.input_condition: Input literal has 'type' as an invalid type name string
_constraints.output_rule: Status must be 'error' (specific error code not defined in tests, but implied by 'type_mismatch' or generic error upon validation failure).

_constraints.input_condition: Input literal has 'type' set to arbitrary string that is not a valid type
_constraints.output_rule: Status must be 'error'.

_constraints.input_condition: Filter condition compares a column to a constant where the column has a null
_constraints.output_rule: The row containing the null must NOT be included in the indices list.

_constraints.input_condition: Projection evaluates an 'if' node where one branch depends on the other being compared to a null
_constraints.output_rule: The output value must be 'null'.
